bullets.text: Dutch auction tender offer repurchased 16.9 million shares at $88 per share for $1.5 billion, reducing shares outstanding by approximately 7.8% from 217 million.
Drive Insurance from Progressive launched September 21 as a dedicated brand for independent agents; formal market launch set for December 10, targeting 80%-plus of auto insurance sold through agents.
Year-to-date industry auto segment grew an estimated 4%; Progressive Direct was the fastest-growing top-15 insurer at 17% and Agency ranked sixth at 11%.
Overall new business pace matched prior year; year-over-year percentage growth slowed significantly from prior three years, reflecting soft market conditions and rate stability.
Four hurricanes hit Florida and Southeast; Progressive closed 94% of hurricane-related claims and reached approximately 80% closed within 20–25 days of each storm.
Concierge claims strategy met expansion performance standards; multiple new service center locations to be announced with planned openings in 2005.
Retention measures unexpectedly declined during the quarter despite rate stability; management flagged understanding retention as a key ongoing challenge.
Progressive and other insurers received subpoenas related to industry-wide bid-rigging investigations; management stated it is not a target and will cooperate fully.
